$comment: Framework-owned paths synced into downstream projects by scripts/sync-framework.ts. `dir: true` copies the directory recursively. `skipIfExists: true` never overwrites an existing target file. `adapt` marks files that need per-project reconciliation after a sync. `templateRepo` lets a downstream repo self-audit: `node scripts/sync-framework.ts --check` clones it and compares.

files.adapt: contains project-specific text (coverage-floor wording, project notes) — use this as the new base and reapply the target's deltas on top
only the header/format is framework-owned; a target's entries are its history and must never be clobbered — the file is skipped whenever it already exists

files.$comment: Fully framework-owned — no `adapt` note because downstream customization is not supported. A sync silently overwrites it; downstream-specific guidance belongs in the target's own docs (README/PREFERENCES.md), never here.

files.adapt: the jq one-liners and signal tables are generic, but §0's doc/tool routing may reference project-specific skills — reconcile if the target has customized its commands
a target's preference bullets are the user's customizations and must never be clobbered — the file is skipped whenever it already exists
coverage floor is per-project — the numbers live in scripts/gates.ts (COVERAGE_FLOOR); restore the target's floor after a sync
entry/project globs and ignoreDependencies grow with project-specific folders (e.g. an api/ dir) — merge the target's additions instead of overwriting
COVERAGE_FLOOR is per-project — restore the target's floor after a sync
